---
title: "Workshop Introduction"
subtitle: "Using Generative and Agentic AI <br> for Omics Data Analysis" 
date: 2026-07-13
author:
  - name: Jelmer Poelstra
    affiliation: 
      - name: Lead,<br> CFAES Bioinformatics Core
        url: https://go.osu.edu/bioinformatics-core
  - name: Menuka Bhandari
    affiliation: 
      - name: Bioinformatician,<br> CFAES Bioinformatics Core
        url: https://go.osu.edu/bioinformatics-core
  - name: Hannah Toth
    affiliation: 
      - name: Postdoc,<br> Department of Plant Pathology
        url: https://plantpath.osu.edu/
  - name: Jonathan Jacobs
    affiliation: 
      - name: Faculty,<br> Department of Plant Pathology
        url: https://plantpath.osu.edu/
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "silver"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme_mod.scss]
    css: slides.css
    smaller: true
    scrollable: false
    progress: true
    incremental: false
    slide-number: true
    slide-level: 2
    highlight-style: pygments
    embed-resources: false # Can't be used with chalkboard
    chalkboard:
      theme: whiteboard
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 1
    toc-title: Overview
    footer: "[~ GenAI-workshop ~](https://cfaes-bioinfo.github.io/agentic-ai-workshop/)"
    logo: img/CFAES-Horiz-RGBHEX.svg
    menu:
      width: wide
      numbers: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
bibliography: refs.bib
---

# Instructor introductions {background-color="silver"}

# What is the workshop about? {background-color="silver"}

## The general idea

- Focus on **general-purpose AI** in the form of LLMs **to help you write code** --- \
  code then used to do the data analysis

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

. . .

::: callout-caution
### We are **not**:

::: incremental

- Asking the LLMs to do the analysis for us:
  - not directly, e.g. "summarize these 15 books"
  - nor indirectly, e.g. by making it run everything for us

- Using specialized AI applications like AlphaFold, Evo2, and so on
:::
:::

## The general idea (cont.)

- Rather, focus on improving how you interact with LLMs:

  - From web-based chat ~ "_email conversation with a smart person_"
  - To in-editor, agentic chat ~ "_having them sit next to you, coding while you talk_"

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

. . .

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This is very general, so:\
while example data is omics data, principles and tooling apply to any data type
:::

. . .

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

::: callout-warning
## We are not AI experts

- Just practitioners that are --hopefully!-- a few steps ahead of you
- _(A lot of AI-for-code content is aimed at (aspiring) software developers.)_
:::

---

![](/img/genAI_tasks.png){width="100%"}

---

![](/img/genAI_tools.png)

## Infrastructure

- Highly practical, many hands-on sessions:
  follow along with us, and try things yourself

- Core infrastructure during hands-on work:
  - Own installation of VS Code (_you should have this installed_)
  - The Ohio Supercomputer (OSC)
  - In-editor AI tools (GitHub Copilot & Claude Code)

. . .

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

::: {.callout-tip}
#### Setup/configuration

- We will spend significant time on setup steps
  [☹️]{style="font-size: 1.4em; font-style: normal"}
- This is one-time setup that you can continue to use after the workshop
  [😃]{style="font-size: 1.4em; font-style: normal"}
:::

## Examples and data

- Examples will mainly:
  - Use an RNA-Seq dataset of rice infected with a fungus from @iqbal2025
  - Involve working on shell scripts to run command-line tools

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

. . .

- Your freedom:
  - Tomorrow afternoon for DIY with any dataset you like
  - Feeling on top of things?
    Free to try example prompts with other data or intentions in other sessions, too
  - More of an R person? We have a [Positron setup page](o3-positron-install.qmd)!

. . .

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
You don't need to have your own dataset:\
can also continue working on the example dataset
:::

---

![](/img/data_types.png)

# The schedule {background-color="silver"}

## Day 1: Monday, July 13

::: {style="font-size: 0.85em;"}

| Section | Time | Instructor | Topic & link |
|:-:|----|---|------------|
| 1 | 09:00 am -- 09:45 am | _all_ | [Intro to the workshop](./01-workshop-intro.qmd) |
| 2 | 09:45 am -- 10:45 am | Menuka | [Intro to Generative AI and LLMs](./02-ai-intro.qmd) |
|  | [*10:45 am -- 11:00 am*]{style="color:steelblue"} |  | ☕️ [*Break*]{style="color:steelblue"} |
| 3 | 11:00 am -- 11:30 am | Derek Ditmer, OTDI | Approved genAI Tools at OSU |
| 4 | 11:30 am -- 12:15 pm | Jonathan | GenAI use cases and tools for research |
|  | [*12:15 pm -- 01:00 pm*]{style="color:steelblue"} |  | 🍽 [*Lunch*]{style="color:steelblue"} |
| 5 | 01:00 pm -- 02:00 pm | Jelmer | [OSC and VS Code setup](./05-setup.qmd) |
|  | [*02:00 pm -- 02:15 pm*]{style="color:steelblue"} |  | ☕️ [*Break*]{style="color:steelblue"} |
| 6 | 02:15 pm -- 03:15 pm | Jelmer | [Code completions with GitHub Copilot](./06-copilot.qmd) |
|  | [*03:15 pm -- 03:30 pm*]{style="color:steelblue"} |  | ☕️ [*Break*]{style="color:steelblue"} |
| 7 | 03:30 pm -- 04:00 pm | Evan Jaffe, OSC | LLM options at OSC |

:::

## Day 2: Tuesday, July 14

::: {style="font-size: 0.85em;"}

| Section | Time | Instructor | Topic & link |
|:-:|----|---|------------|
| 8 | 09:00 am -- 10:00 am | Jelmer | [Agentic chat with Claude Code](./08-claude.qmd) |
| 9 | 10:00 am -- 10:30 am | Hannah | Prompt engineering |
|  | [*10:30 am -- 10:45 am*]{style="color:steelblue"} |  | ☕️ [*Break*]{style="color:steelblue"} |
| 10 | 10:45 am -- 11:45 am | Menuka | Advanced agentic options and skills |
| 11 | 11:45 am -- 12:15 pm | Hannah | Limitations and responsible use of AI |
|  | [*12:15 pm -- 01:00 pm*]{style="color:steelblue"} |  | 🍽 [*Lunch*]{style="color:steelblue"} |
| 13 | 01:00 pm -- 03:30 pm | *all* | DIY with own (or other) data |
| 14 | 03:30 pm -- 04:00 pm | *all* | Concluding remarks and looking forward |

:::

# How to ask questions and contribute {background-color="silver"}

## How to ask questions and contribute

- Don't hesitate to interrupt if you have a **question**

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

. . .

- Same if you want to **share your experience** with the group ---\
  you may have a different perspective, a better solution, etc.!

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

. . .

- If you're **stuck, behind, or similar**, always let us know ---\
  use your best judgement whether to interrupt or to put up hand /
  use a sticky note / use the Zoom chat

## Sticky notes

We have sticky notes in 10 colors:

- Use [**dark olive-green**]{style="color: darkolivegreen;"} to say "_I need a break within the next 30 seconds._"

. . .

- Use [**medium sea-green**]{style="color: mediumseagreen;"} to say "_I am re-watching my favorite episode of Friends, but please carry on._"

. . .

- Use [**cornflower blue**]{style="color: cornflowerblue;"} to say "_I already asked ChatGPT and it disagreed with you_."

## Sticky notes (take 2)

- Use [**blue**]{style="color: blue;"} to say: "_Slow down / I am falling behind_"

- Use [**red**]{style="color: red;"} to say "_I have a (non-urgent) issue_"

## Final pieces of information

- Coffee/tea, snacks, and lunch will be provided during breaks

- Forgetting anything?

<br>

::: callout-note
#### Links
- Website: <https://cfaes-bioinfo.github.io/agentic-ai-workshop>
- [Zoom](https://osu.zoom.us/j/95965101793?pwd=bi2akCYEUb19TpNhGbU9EtNgfala5a.1)
:::

# Questions? {background-color="silver"}

## References

<!----

# Brief participant introductions {background-color="silver"}

## Brief participant introductions

- Name
- Position and department
- Your most satisfying or frustrating genAI experience
- Who should win the world cup

---->
